Items.Title: 为什么讨厌 CSS？ | Why is CSS Hated On?
Items.Description:
加布里埃尔·佩德罗萨

发表于我看过很多关于人们鄙视 css 的帖子，但我不明白为什么。 是不是基础不够？ 是特性的特殊性还是丰富性？ 如果你们愿意，我可以创建一个多部分的 CSS 系列，从 CSS 的绝对基础到高级属性，或者我可以在上面创建一个巨大的备忘单作为简单的书签。 在评论中让我知道您对 CSS 的看法，我应该创建一篇关于它的深入帖子或备忘单吗？



原文:

Gabriel Pedroza

Posted on May 31

I've seen a lot of posts about people despising css but I don't truly understand why. Is it the lack of foundation? Is it the specificity or the abundance of properties? If you guys want, I can create a multi-part series of CSS starting from the absolute basics to the advanced properties of CSS or I can create a huge cheat sheet on it for an easy bookmark. Let me know what you think of CSS in the comments and should I create an in-depth post or cheat sheet about it?

Items.Title: 过去一周的 7 大特色 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
Items.Description:
每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从周一开始，到周日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

令人兴奋的 HTML 技巧

看看这 8 个有用的、鲜为人知的 HTML 技巧！ 支持@babib分享如此方便的列表和简明的解释。

颤振 101

探索 F

在过去的几年里，寓言变得非常有能力！

正则表达式大师是 JS！

当您第一次遇到正则表达式时，它们可能看起来像是一串随机的乱码。 虽然它们可能看起来很尴尬（语法有些混乱），但它们也非常有用。 通过@kai_wenzel了解更多关于它们的信息！

完整的 Windows 开发人员设置指南

曾经想在 Windows 中进行开发吗？ 经过大量的试验和错误， @stephanlamoureux已经能够拼凑出一个相当受人尊敬的 Windows 开发环境。

如何构建 React 应用程序

只要 React 存在，“以正确的方式”构建 React 应用程序似乎就是热门话题。 好吧，在 React 中工作的开发人员当然可以，这里是@adevnadia的！

终端式投资组合

并非所有最伟大的产品组合都拥有最闪亮的造型或最新的尖端技术。 这是@protium的个人资料网站：只是一个简单的终端！

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 本周关注 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注这个系列。 你可能只是在里面！



原文:

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

Mind-blowing HTML Tips

Check out these 8 helpful, lesser-known HTML tips! Props to @babib for sharing such a handy list with concise explanations.

Flutter 101

Flutter, an open source framework by Google, is great for building beautiful, multi-platform apps from a single codebase. @dmutoni helps you get started with Flutter in this guide!

Exploring F

@tunaxor talks about what the current landscape of front-end development for the F# ecosystem looks like. Over the last few years Fable has become quite capable!

Master RegEx is JS!

When you first encounter Regular Expressions, they may seem like a random string of gibberish. While they might look awkward (with a somewhat confusing syntax), they are also extremely useful. Learn more about them with @kai_wenzel!

Complete Windows Developer Setup Guide

Ever wanted to develop in Windows? After a lot of trial and error, @stephanlamoureux has been able to piece together a pretty respectable Windows dev environment.

How to Structure React Apps

Structuring React apps “the right way” seems to be the hot topic for as long as React has existed. React’s official opinion on it is that it “doesn’t have opinions.” Well, devs working in React certainly do, and here’s @adevnadia’s!

Terminal-style Portfolio

Not all the greatest portfolios have the shiniest styling or the latest cutting-edge technology. Here’s @protium’s profile website: just a simple terminal!

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Items.Title: CTO 最后一天：反思、错误和一些学习 | CTO last day: reflections, mistakes, and some learnings
Items.Description:
在担任该职位大约两年后，我于 2021 年 4 月辞去了在 Akvo 担任首席技术官的工作。

今天， 六七八九十个月后，似乎是时候停止拖延并写点什么了。

离开

当周日晚上成为一周中最糟糕的部分时，我意识到我需要离开，那时我的感觉与我以前的学生时代完全一样：焦虑和不开心。

但这不仅仅是周日晚上。 我从不介意在工作时间之外突然出现一些工作问题——我喜欢解决问题——但现在它只会吸走我所做的所有事情的所有乐趣。

当然，这一切带来的睡眠问题。

真正的幸福只有在您发现自己喜欢的问题并乐于解决时才会出现。
马克·曼森（Mark Manson），不给 F*ck 的微妙艺术。

根本原因分析

无需通过5 个为什么来查看原因：公司财务状况的复合效应、我参与的决策的影响以及完全没有准备的感觉。

例如，我对新业务战略的翻译：停止产品开发。

自十二年前公司成立以来一直是该公司的旗舰产品，我该推动谁停止开发？ 更有经验的 CTO 会不会有不同的看法？ 更有经验的 CTO 会改变业务战略吗？ 改变了产品开发的融资方式？ 合同是如何谈判的？ 寻找外部投资？

以及对开发团队的影响……

这是我帮助做出的最后一个决定。

错误和教训

强制性“经验教训”部分：

  1. 再见编码。
  2. 投诉很好。
  3. 清晰是一项艰苦的工作。
  4. 业务第一，技术第二。
  5. 冲突：避免成为坏掉的电话。
  6. 艰难的对话（有数据）。
  7. 始终是系统问题。
  8. 自责是没有意义的。
  9. 这都是关于团队的。
  10. 不要进行更改，进行实验。
  11. 反馈循环很长。
  12. 围栏是必要的邪恶。
  13. 孤独。
  14. 我不讨厌人事管理！

再见编码

尽管很痛苦，但总会有比编码更重要的事情。

投诉很好

不是所有的抱怨，也不是一直抱怨，但一定程度的抱怨意味着人们足够信任你，他们对工作足够关心。

投诉是一个机会：

  1. 做出改进。
  2. 提供清晰度。

最好的结果是在给予一些额外的明确说明后委托投诉者的改进。

清晰是一项艰苦的工作

不仅沟通是混乱的，而且你需要重复自己十次，首先要自己找到清晰的内容需要大量的工作。

业务第一，技术第二

我明白我作为 CTO 的主要职责是改进我们交付软件的方式。 重大错误。

我应该从一开始就深入研究业务如何运作以及软件如何支持和启用它。

冲突：避免成为坏掉的电话

您与 Bar 谈论 Buzz，但 Bar 说 Quux，您对此一无所知。 因此，您回到 Foo 以澄清 Bar 的 Buzz 的 Quux 和 Foo 回复 Flob。

多么浪费时间、精力和混乱的根源。

根本问题是 Foo 不想与 Bar 进行艰难的对话，而我对此深表同情，这需要发生。

因此，根据情况：

  1. 让 Foo 与 Bar 进行一次艰难的对话。
  2. 与 Foo 和 Bar 坐在一起进行艰难的对话。

困难的对话（有数据）

有两个因素使艰难的对话变得不那么困难：

总是系统问题

在目前的情况下，每个人都在尽力而为。
考虑到这一点，一旦你从责备他人转变为理解、检查和改变系统，对话就会变得更加富有成效。

步骤：

  1. 说明我们正试图了解系统如何导致结果。
  2. 重复第 1 点。
  3. 同意结果是不可取的。
  4. 收集有关系统行为的时间线/事实，以了解“情况”。
  5. 就理想的结果达成一致。
  6. 同意需要哪些系统更改来避免（1）和促进（2）。

请注意，“情况”可能是工作人员缺乏知识或能力。 这是可以修复的。

改变系统，人们就会跟随。

自责是没有意义的

我一直认为承认“这是我的错！” 这样做是一件勇敢的事，但如果你真的相信某件事是你的错，那么你就准备好相信其他人也有过错。

责备和自责会适得其反。 始终是系统问题。

一切都与团队有关

什么团队、组成、职责、内部和外部动态……这将是您进行系统更改的主要杠杆点。

不要进行更改，进行实验

变化是可怕的，并伴随着很多阻力，但进行实验会发出完全不同的信号：这是一个旨在学习的暂时性事物。

实验预计会“失败”，这带来了诚实反馈所需的心理安全，并增加了参与的意愿。

反馈是高质量的，因为它来自经验证据，而不是理论上的“这在这里永远行不通”。

而最重要的原因：

改变文化首先要改变人们的行为，而不是人们的想法。
反馈循环很长

你会错过那些 72 小时的测试套件。

围栏是必要的邪恶

我犯了好几次错误，要求人们做重要但不紧急的工作，并期望他们能抽出时间去做。 它很少奏效。

尽管让我很痛苦，但解决方法是明确限制人们的时间，这样如果出现其他冲突的优先级，他们可以依靠你的权威来推回，或者将冲突升级给你。

孤独

我从来没有这么多话，开过这么多会议，但你们公司里没有人会和你们一样，所以我有时真的很孤独。

我最终在 LinkedIn 上联系了随机的 CTO，这非常有用。

我不讨厌人事管理！

你可能不在乎，但这对我来说是一个很大的惊喜。

即使 1-2-1 天真的很累，我真的很享受与其他人力资源共度的时光。

接下来是什么？

这种体验就像我的第一杯啤酒、第一篇博文或第一次会议演讲。

尝试一次还不足以让我下定决心。

但在开始新工作之前，最初的计划是在 2021 年剩下的时间里休息，从工作和 COVID 中得到喘息，并在我妻子经历一些令人讨厌的手术时照顾我的家人。

但生活还有其他计划......



原文:

In April 2021, I left my job at Akvo as CTO, after about two years in the position.

Today, six seven eight nine ten months later, it seems like about time to stop procrastinating and write something about it.

Leaving

I realised that I needed to leave when Sunday evenings became the worst part of the week when I felt exactly the same as in my old school days: anxious and unhappy.

But it was not only Sunday evenings. I have never minded some work problem popping in my head out of working hours -- I love solving problems -- but now it will just suck all the joy of whatever I was doing.

And of course, the sleeping troubles that came with all of this.

True happiness occurs only when you find the problems you enjoy having and enjoy solving.
Mark Manson, The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck.

Root Cause Analysis

No need to go through the 5 whys to see what the cause was: the compounded effect of the company’s financial situation, the impact of the decisions I was involved with, and the feeling of being completely unprepared.

As an example, my translation of the new business strategy: stop product development.

Who I was to push for halting the development of what has been the company’s flagship product since its inception, twelve years ago? Would a more experienced CTO see otherwise? Would a more experienced CTO change the business strategy? Changed how product development was financed? How contracts were negotiated? Find external investment?

And the impact on the development team …

This was the last decision that I helped make.

Mistakes and lessons learned

Mandatory “lessons learned” section:

  1. Bye-bye coding.
  2. Complaints are good.
  3. Clarity is hard work.
  4. Business first, tech second.
  5. Conflicts: avoid being a broken telephone.
  6. Difficult conversations (with data).
  7. It is always a system problem.
  8. Self-blaming is pointless.
  9. It is all about teams.
  10. Don't make changes, run experiments.
  11. Feedback loops are loooong.
  12. Ring-fencing is a necessary evil.
  13. Lonely.
  14. I don’t dislike people management!

Bye-bye coding

As much as it hurts, there will be always something more important than coding.

Complaints are good

Not all complains and not all the time, but some level of complaining means that people trust you enough and that they care enough about the job.

Complains are an opportunity to either:

  1. Make an improvement.
  2. Provide clarity.

Best outcome is delegating the improvement in the complainer after giving some additional clarity.

Clarity is hard work

Not only communication is messy and you will need to repeat yourself ten times, finding the clarity yourself in the first place requires a lot of work.

Business first, tech second

I understood that my main responsibility as CTO was to improve how we delivered software. Major mistake.

I should have dug deeper from the very beginning on how the business works and how software supported and enabled it.

Conflicts: avoid being a broken telephone

Foo complains about Bar because of Buzz. You talk with Bar about Buzz but Bar says Quux, which you have no clue about. So you go back to Foo to clarify Bar's Buzz's Quux and Foo replies Flob.

What a waste of time, energy, and a source of confusion.

The underlying issue is that Foo does not want to have a difficult conversation with Bar, which, as much I can empathise with, it needs to happen.

So either, depending on circumstances:

  1. Ask Foo to have a difficult conversation with Bar.
  2. Sit with Foo and Bar to have a difficult conversation.

Difficult conversations (with data)

Two ingredients that made difficult conversations less difficult to have:

It is always a system problem

Everybody is doing their best, under the current circumstances.
Gerald M. Weinberg, Becoming a Technical Leader

With this in mind, it is amazing how much more productive conversations are once you move from blaming people to understanding, inspecting, and changing the system.

The steps:

  1. State that we are trying to understand how the system leads to the outcome.
  2. Repeat point 1.
  3. Agree that the outcome was undesirable.
  4. Collect the timeline/facts about the system behavior, to understand "the circumstances".
  5. Agree on what was the desirable outcome.
  6. Agree on what system change is needed to avoid (1) and promote (2).

Note that "circumstances" can be a lack of knowledge or competency by the person doing the work. This is something that can be fixed.

Change the system and people will follow.

Self-blaming is pointless

I always thought that acknowledging that "It is my fault!" was a brave thing to do, but if you really believe that something is your fault, then you are ready to believe that others can be at fault.

Blaming and self-blaming are counterproductive. It is always a system problem.

It is all about teams

What teams, composition, responsibilities, the internal and external dynamics, ... this is going to be your main leverage point for system change.

Don't make changes, run experiments

Change is scary and comes with a lot of resistance, but running an experiment sends a different completely different signal: it is a temporal thing aimed to learn.

Experiments are expected to "fail", which brings the psychological safety required for honest feedback and increases the willingness to participate.

Feedback is high quality as it comes from empirical evidence, not theoretical "this will never work here".

And the most important reason:

Change culture by first changing what people do, not how people think.
John Shook, How to Change a Culture: Lessons from NUMMI

Feedback loops are loooong

You will miss those 72 hours test suites.

Ring-fencing is a necessary evil

I made several times the mistake of asking people for important-but-not-urgent work and expecting them to find the time for it. It rarely worked.

As much as it pains me, the workaround is to explicitly ring-fence people's time, so that if other conflicting priority arises, they can rely on your authority to push back, or escalate the conflict to you.

Lonely

I have never talked so much and been in so many meetings, but nobody in your company will be in your same position, so I actually felt quite lonely at times.

I ended up reaching out to random CTOs on LinkedIn, which was surprisingly useful.

I don’t dislike people management!

You probably do not care but it was a big surprise for me.

Even if the 1-2-1 days were really really draining, I actually enjoyed the time spent with other human resources.

What is next?

The experience was like my first beer, first blog post or first conference talk.

Trying once is not enough to make up my mind.

But before starting a new job, the original plan was to take the rest of 2021 off, get a respite from work and COVID, and take care of my family while my wife went through some nasty surgery.

But life had other plans ...

Items.Title: 自定义我的 Zsh 提示 | Customizing my Zsh Prompt
Items.Description:
我有一个小.zshrc文件来自定义我的终端，除了默认的提示之外，我一直想要一个有趣的提示，但并没有真正学习它。

因此，经过一些研究，我弄清楚了如何使我的小提示有用而又不会过于压倒或分散注意力的基础知识！

这是它的样子：

你可能会注意到我没有典型的东西，比如我的用户名或机器名或任何东西，因为......我个人不需要。 但是，阅读本文后，您将能够自己添加！

输入什么

如果您不知道如何打开配置文件，请继续使用vim ~/.zshrc或code ~/.zshrc （或您喜欢的任何编辑器）打开它。

首先，将这两行添加到您的.zshrc中：

 autoload -Uz vcs_info precmd () { vcs_info }

这会将版本控制系统加载到您的提示符中（这是它的文档）。

接下来，您希望将设置 Git 分支详细信息的下一行添加到提示符中。

 zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '%b '

那%b是分支名称变量。 出于格式原因，我在其后添加了一个空格。 您可以在字符串中添加其他变量（这里有一个很好的选项博客），例如有关存储的信息、存储库的根目录的名称、分阶段的更改等。

现在，您将添加最后两行以将提示放在一起！

 setopt PROMPT_SUBST PROMPT = '%F{green}%*%f %F{blue}%~%f %F{red}${vcs_info_msg_0_}%f$ '

这看起来很毛茸茸，所以让我分解一下。

颜色！

当您将事物包围在%F和%f中时，这会改变它们之间任何事物的前景色。 在这种情况下，您可以看到绿色、蓝色和红色。 日期和时间！

您看到的%*是时间（以 24 小时格式，以秒为单位）。 对此还有更多选项，例如日期的%W 。 文档在这里，滚动到“13.2.4 日期和时间”。

目录！

如果您想用其他东西替换它，请查看文档中的“13.2.3 Shell state”。

变数！

现在下一部分正在加载您的变量${vcs_info_msg_0_} 。 这会弹出我们之前在红色文本包装器中创建的分支变量。

最后的$只是为了引导用户输入。 您可以将其替换为>或; 或WOW或更多变量，或任何你想要的。

结束！

这是您可以粘贴和自定义的整个提示：

 autoload -Uz vcs_info precmd () { vcs_info } zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '%b ' setopt PROMPT_SUBST PROMPT = '%F{green}%*%f %F{blue}%~%f %F{red}${vcs_info_msg_0_}%f$ '

保存后，运行source ~/.zshrc将其加载到终端中（或者只需重新启动终端/打开一个新选项卡）。 因为我喜欢快速打开和编辑，所以我添加了这些别名以使打开+加载更快：

 alias bp = 'vim ~/.zshrc' alias sa = 'source ~/.zshrc;echo "ZSH aliases sourced."'

而现在世界的 ya oystah。 我希望这对你有帮助！



原文:

I have a little .zshrc file to customize my terminal, and I've always wanted a fun prompt besides the default, but haven't really done much to learn it.

So, after some research, I figured out the basics of how to make my little prompt useful without being too overwhelming or distracting!

Here's what it looks like:

You might notice that I don't have the typical things like my username or machine name or anything, because... I don't need that personally. But, after reading this, you'll be able to add that yourself!

What to type

If you don't know how to open your configuration file, go ahead and open it with vim ~/.zshrc or code ~/.zshrc (or whatever editor your prefer).

First, add these two lines to your .zshrc:

autoload -Uz vcs_info
precmd() { vcs_info }

This loads the Version Control System into your prompt (here's the documentation for it).

Next, you want to add the next line that sets up the Git branch details into your prompt.

zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '%b '

That %b is the branch name variable. I added a space after it for formatting reasons. You can add other variables in the string there (here's a good blog of options), for example information about stashes, the name of the root directory of the repo, staged changes, etc.

Now, you'll add these final two lines to put your prompt together!

setopt PROMPT_SUBST
PROMPT='%F{green}%*%f %F{blue}%~%f %F{red}${vcs_info_msg_0_}%f$ '

This is hairy-looking, so let me break it down.

Colors!

When you surround things in %F and %f, that changes the foreground color of anything in between them. The color in the {curly braces} is the color that's edited. In this case, you can see green, blue, and red. There's similar things like this with %U and %u to underline, %K and %k for background colors, and more (with documentation here, scroll to "13.2.5 Visual effects"). ZSH understands the colors black, red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan and white by default!

Dates and times!

The %* that you see is the time (in 24-hour format, with seconds). There's more options for this, like %W for the date, for example. The docs for this is here, scroll to "13.2.4 Date and time".

The directory!

The %~ shows the current working directory, and its path. Look at "13.2.3 Shell state" in the docs if you want to replace it with something else.

The variable!

Now the next part is loading in your variable, the ${vcs_info_msg_0_}. This plops your branch variable that we made before in that red text wrapper, there.

The final $ at the end is just to lead the user input. You can replace it with > or ; or WOW or more variables, or whatever you'd like.

The end!

Here is the whole prompt that you can paste in and customize:

autoload -Uz vcs_info
precmd() { vcs_info } zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '%b ' setopt PROMPT_SUBST
PROMPT='%F{green}%*%f %F{blue}%~%f %F{red}${vcs_info_msg_0_}%f$ '

Once you've saved this, run source ~/.zshrc to load it in the terminal (or just restart your terminal/open a new tab). Because I like opening and editing quickly, I added these aliases to make the opening + loading faster:

alias bp='vim ~/.zshrc'
alias sa='source ~/.zshrc;echo "ZSH aliases sourced."'

And now the world's ya oystah. I hope this was helpful for you!

Items.Title: 编码和多动症 - 无法开始 | Coding and ADHD - Can't Start
Items.Description:
我想开始任务。 我知道我需要开始任务。 为什么我不能开始任务？

耻辱

多动症的大脑收到了太多关于他们完成任务能力的负面信息，以至于无法开始往往源于羞耻。 我们中的许多人都在与完美主义作斗争，没有人喜欢在同一任务上反复尝试和失败。

我强烈推荐 How to ADHD 和 Brendan Mahan 的可怕解释之墙第 1部分和第 2 部分。 我发现非常有用的一本短书是如何在溺水时保持房屋。 我发布了关于我的 ADHD 的推文并关注了许多关于他们的 ADHD 的推文，因为知道其他人也在与这些事情作斗争是非常有帮助的。

压倒

多动症患者经常发现配对/共同工作/身体加倍有帮助，尤其是在开始一项任务时。 我必须找到一个平衡点，因为我会在有机会的时候偷听别人的耳朵，这不利于完成我的工作。

在我的一生中，当我遇到一个我不想开始的大任务时，对我有用的主要事情就是写下所有的步骤。 我经常发现我对某件事感到不知所措，因为当我实际上是 20 岁时，我认为它是一个步骤。

接下来，我将每个步骤分解成最小的步骤。 然后我在该列表中找到最小的步骤并尝试它。 对于代码，有时这就像制作一个空白文件并导入一些东西一样简单。 一旦我完成它，我的大脑就会宣布任务开始，突然间事情变得不那么压倒性了。

这对于患有多动症的人来说可能非常困难，所以永远不要害怕寻求帮助来打破它。 这可能是开发工作适合多动症患者的一个领域。 讨论任务中涉及的各个步骤以正确估计它们并确定它们的优先级是正常的。 通常会有一个人的工作是帮助您这样做，如果没有，与另一个开发人员配对可能是一件好事。 如果您的公司内没有这样的开发人员，您可以请朋友或加入网络组。

一点点

我最近了解到的是，投入大量时间尝试这项任务或一小部分任务确实对 ADHD 大脑有效。 或者“我只做这个重构的 5%”通常足以让我们开始。 一旦这 5% 完成，这种势头通常会提供足够的动力来开始另一个 5%，依此类推。

在一天工作结束时，我经常会问自己是否有一些小任务我现在可以做，以使早上更容易开始。 对我来说，这通常是为了确保我明天的待办事项清单上有我想在早上尝试的第一个任务，以及我所有的会议/约会/活动。 有时，它会整理办公桌上的杂物以限制分心，或者在帖子上给自己写一个鼓励性的便条。

在我坐在沙发上放松之前，我试着问自己明天我能做些什么小任务来帮助自己正常工作。 例如，我是否可以在 3 步或更少的时间内准备好早餐？ 如果没有，我应该准备一些。 我正在努力寻找在我有动力的时候能做的最小的任务，而不是希望我早上有动力。

结论

我错过了您喜欢的资源或提示吗？ 发表评论！

快来了：

  • 无法继续
  • 停不下来
  • 不记得


原文:

I want to start the task. I know I need to start the task. Why can't I start the task?

Shame

ADHD brains have received so much negative messaging about their ability to do tasks, that inability to start is often rooted in shame. Many of us struggle with perfectionism, and no one enjoys trying and failing at the same task repeatedly.

I highly recommend How to ADHD and Brendan Mahan's Wall of Awful explanation part 1 and part 2. A short book I found very helpful is How to Keep House While Drowning. Jesse J. Anderson also does a great job of explaining how ADHD brains are motivated and some tools to use in this video. Black Girl Lost Keys has tons of helpful resources. I tweet about my ADHD and follow many people who tweet about their ADHD because it is very helpful to know other people are struggling with these things too.

Overwhelm

ADHD people often find pairing/co-working/body doubling helpful, especially for starting a task. I have to find a balance because I will chat someone's ear off given the opportunity, to the detriment of getting my work done.

Throughout my life, the main thing that has worked for me when I encounter a large task I don't want to start is writing down all the steps. Often I find I'm overwhelmed by something because I'm thinking of it as one step when it's actually 20.

Next I break each of those steps down into its smallest steps. Then I find the smallest step in that list and try it. For code, sometimes this is as easy as making a blank file and importing something. Once I've done it, my brain declares the task started, and suddenly things are much less overwhelming.

This can be very difficult for people with ADHD, so never be afraid to ask for help breaking it down. This can be one area in which development work suits people with ADHD. It is normal to discuss the individual steps involved in a task in order to properly estimate and prioritize them. There is often a person whose job it is to help you do so, and if not, it could be a great thing to pair with another developer on. If there's not a developer like that within your company, you can ask a friend or join a networking group.

Just a Little Bit

Something I've learned recently is that committing to an amount of time trying the task or a small percent of the task really works for ADHD brains. "I'll do 15 minutes of dishes." or "I'll just do 5% of this refactor" is often enough to get us started. Once that 5% is complete, that momentum often gives enough motivation to start another 5% and so on.

At the end of the work day, I'll often ask myself if there are small tasks I can do right now to make it easier to start in the morning. For me, this is usually making sure my to do list for tomorrow has the first task I want to try in the morning and all my meetings/appointments/events on it. Sometimes it's picking up desk clutter to limit distractions or writing myself an encouraging note on a post it.

Before I sit on the couch to relax, I try to ask myself what small task I can do to help myself function tomorrow. For example, do I have breakfast I can prep in 3 steps or less? If not, I should prep some. I'm trying to find the smallest task I can do while I have some momentum, instead of hoping I have the motivation in the morning.

Conclusion

Did I miss a resource or tip you love? Leave a comment!

Items.Title: 在 JavaScript 中操作数组 | Manipulating Arrays in JavaScript
Items.Description:
珍贵的龙

发表于数组操作是指可以对它们执行的大量操作，例如添加到已声明的数组、从中删除或以各种方式更改它。 而这些活动可以通过使用方法来进行。

每个 JavaScript 开发人员都必须在某些时候与数组交互，我们将在这里讨论可以修改这些数组的各种方式。
在本教程中，您将逐步学习如何使用各种方法修改数组。

什么是数组以及如何使用它们？

数组是一个变量，可以包含各种数据类型的元素，例如字符串（“”）、数字（0....infinity）、布尔值（true/false）等。数组中没有数据类型区分。 这些元素保存在这个变量中，可以随时访问。 让我们从声明我们的数组开始。 

 var arrayName = []; //Initial array declaration var resources = [ " chrome " , " google " , " notion " , " youtube " ];

第一行声明一个空数组，而第二行声明一个元素数组。

数组操作方法

现在让我们开始工作并解释一些可供我们使用的各种方法。 如前所述，我们可以使用方法来执行一些特定的任务，例如添加和删除。 因此，我们将从执行此操作的方法开始。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; //remember an array can contain any datatype

推（）

此方法用于在数组末尾添加一个新元素，从而改变数组的长度。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . push ( " New-Element " ); console . log ( arr ); //This will leave the array as ["JS",123,false,"or","today","New-Element"]

流行音乐（）

此方法用于删除数组的最后一个元素，从而改变数组的长度。 它不需要任何参数。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . pop (); //"today" will be removed from the array console . log ( arr ); //['JS', 123, false, 'or']

转移（）

此方法用于删除数组的第一个元素，从而改变数组的长度。 它不需要任何参数。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . shift (); //"JS" will be removed from the array console . log ( arr ); //[123,false,"or","today"]

取消移位（）

此方法用于将元素添加到数组的开头，从而改变数组的长度。 它需要添加新的数组元素作为参数。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . unshift ( " react " ); //This will add "react" to the array console . log ( arr ); //["react","JS",123,false,"or","today"]

我们刚刚讨论了可用于从数组的开头和结尾添加和删除元素的方法。 如果我们愿意，如何从数组的任何部分添加和删除？
让我们看看哪些方法可以帮助我们实现这一目标！！

拼接（）

此方法用于删除、删除和添加或仅将新元素添加到数组中。 我们可以用这种方法实现其中的任何一个。 让我们了解如何实现这一目标。

句法

array . splice ( index , delete - Count , element1 , ..., elementn );

指数：

这指定从哪里开始从数组中删除元素。

删除计数：

此参数指定要从数组中删除的元素数。

元素1 ...，元素：

指定要添加的新元素。 现在让我们看看它的实际效果。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . splice ( 0 , 3 ); //This will remove JS,123 and false from the array console . log ( arr ); //["or","today"]

索引0包含元素["JS"]并且删除计数为3从索引0开始删除三个元素["JS",123,false] 。
上面的代码使用splice()从我们的数组中删除元素，现在让我们删除和替换元素。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . splice ( 2 , 3 , " React " , " Angular " , " Vue " , " Codes " ); //this will remove false,"or" and "today" and add "React","Angular","Vue","Codes" console . log ( arr ) //["JS",123,"React","Angular","Vue","Codes"]

在这里，我们的索引是2 ，其中包含元素[false]并且我们的删除计数是3 。 这会删除从[false]开始的三个元素，并将["React","Angular","Vue","Codes"]添加到数组中。 最后只允许添加元素而不必删除任何元素。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . splice ( 4 , 0 , " React " , " Angular " ); // this adds "React"and "Angular" to the array. console . log () arr //["JS",123,false,"or","today",today"];

所以我们可以从例子中看到，在不同的点，我们可以有不同数量的参数，当 delete-Count 设置为 0 时，不会删除任何元素。 在我们继续之前，请花几分钟再过一遍。

片（）

此方法可用于复制数组中指定数量的元素并将它们分配给新数组。 这不会影响原始数组。

句法

开始：

这指定从哪里开始复制。

结尾：

这指定了复制停止的位置。

让我们看看它是如何工作的。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; arr . slice ( 1 , 4 ); //this will return [123,false,"or","today"] console . log ( arr ); //["JS",123,false,"or","today"]

最佳做法是始终将Slice()分配给新变量

var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; let newArr = arr . slice ( 1 , 4 ); console . log ( newArr ); // [123, false, 'or']

这应该很容易掌握吧？？ 让我们滚动并学习一些其他方法。

此方法用于将数组中的每个元素转换为字符串。 请记住，我们之前了解到数组可以包含任何数据类型。 因此，此方法有助于将数组中的所有数据类型转换为以逗号分隔的字符串。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; console . log ( arr . toString ()); // JS,123,false,or,today

加入（）

这个方法和toString()方法很相似，不同的是这里可​​以指定分隔符。 记住toString用逗号分隔元素，在这里我们可以指定使用什么作为分隔符。

 var arr = [ " JS " , 123 , false , " or " , " today " ]; console . log ( arr . join ( " = " )); //JS=123=false=or=today

连接

此方法用于合并两个数组或将更多元素添加到数组中，然后添加后返回一个新数组。

 var frontEnd = [ " Javascript " , " React " , " Angular " , " Swift " ]; var backEnd = [ " Java " , " Ruby " , " Python " , " PHP " ]; var fullStack = frontEnd . concat ( backEnd ); console . log ( fullStack ); //['Javascript', 'React', 'Angular', 'Swift', 'Java', 'Ruby', 'Python', 'PHP']

结论

在本文中，我们学习了如何使用几种方法在 JavaScript 中操作数组。 我们讨论的这些方法将帮助您在数组上执行简单的任务。 如果您有任何问题，请随时提出问题。

如果您有任何问题或建议，请发表评论或推特给我！ 请务必在社交媒体上关注我！



原文:

Precious Longe

Posted on May 31

Manipulation of arrays refers to the numerous operations that can be performed on them, such as adding to an already declared array, deleting from it, or altering it in various ways. And these activities can be carried out through the use of methods.

Every JavaScript developer must interact with arrays at some time, and we'll go over the various ways these arrays can be modified here.
In this tutorial, you'll learn how to modify arrays using various methods in a step-by-step manner.

What are Arrays and How Do I Use Them?

An array is a variable that can contain elements of various data types, such as strings(" "), numbers (0....infinity), booleans(true/false) etc. There is no datatype discrimination in an array. These elements are saved in this variable, which can be accessed at any time. Let's start by declaring our array.

var arrayName = []; //Initial array declaration
var resources = ["chrome","google","notion","youtube"];

The first line declares an empty array, whereas the second declares an array of elements.

Methods for Array Manipulation

Now let's get to work and explain some of the various methods available to us. As mentioned earlier, we can use methods to carry out some specific tasks such as adding and removing. So we will kick off with the methods for doing that.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"]; //remember an array can contain any datatype

push()

This method is used to add a new element to the end of the array thereby changing the length of the array.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.push("New-Element");
console.log(arr);//This will leave the array as ["JS",123,false,"or","today","New-Element"]

pop()

This method is used to remove the last element of an array thereby changing the length of the array. It does not require any parameters.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.pop();//"today" will be removed from the array
console.log(arr);//['JS', 123, false, 'or']

shift()

This method is used to remove the first element of an array, thereby changing the length of the array. It does not require any parameters.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.shift();//"JS" will be removed from the array
console.log(arr);//[123,false,"or","today"]

unshift()

This method is used to add an element to the beginning of an array, thereby changing the length of the array. It requires the new array element to be added, as a parameter.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.unshift("react");//This will add "react" to the array
console.log(arr);//["react","JS",123,false,"or","today"]

We just discussed the methods that we can use to add and remove elements from both the beginning and end of an array. How about adding and removing from any part of an array if we want to?
Let's look at which method(s) helps us achieve that!!

splice( )

This method is used to remove, remove and add or just add a new element(s) to an array. We can achieve any of these with this method. Let's get to understand how that can be achieved.

Syntax 

array.splice(index, delete-Count, element1, ...,elementn);

Index:

This specifies where to start removing elements from in the array.

delete-Count:

This parameter specifies the number of elements to be removed from an array.

elements1...,elementn:

Specifies the new elements to be added. Now let's see this in action.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.splice(0,3);//This will remove JS,123 and false from the array
console.log(arr);//["or","today"]

The index 0 contains element ["JS"] and the delete-count which is 3 deletes three elements ["JS",123,false] starting from index 0.
The code above is using splice() to remove elements from our array, now lets remove and replace elements.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.splice(2,3,"React","Angular","Vue","Codes");//this will remove false,"or" and "today" and add "React","Angular","Vue","Codes"
console.log(arr)//["JS",123,"React","Angular","Vue","Codes"]

Here, our index is 2, which contains element [false] and our delete-count is 3. This deletes three elements starting from [false] and adds ["React","Angular","Vue","Codes"] to the array. Finally lets only add elements without having to remove any.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.splice(4,0,"React","Angular");// this adds "React"and "Angular" to the array.
console.log()arr//["JS",123,false,"or","today",today"];

So we can see from the examples that at different points, we can have different numbers of parameters and when the delete-Count is set to 0, no element is removed. Take a few minutes to go through that again before we continue.

slice()

This method can be used to copy a specified number(s) of elements in an array and assigns them to a new array. This does not affect the original array.

Syntax

start:

This specifies where to start copying from.

end:

This specifies where the copying stops.

Lets see how that works.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
arr.slice(1,4);//this will return [123,false,"or","today"]
console.log(arr);//["JS",123,false,"or","today"]

Best practice is to always asign the Slice() to a new variable

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
let newArr = arr.slice(1,4);
console.log(newArr);// [123, false, 'or']

That should be easy to grasp right?? Let's roll and learn some other methods.

toString()

This methods is used to convert every element in an array to strings. Remember we learnt earlier that an array can contain any datatype. So this method helps convert all datatypes in an array to strings separated by a comma.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
console.log(arr.toString());// JS,123,false,or,today

join()

This method is very similar to the toString() method, the difference is that here you can specify the separator. Remember toString separates the elements with a comma, here we are able to specify what to use as our separator.

var arr = ["JS",123,false,"or","today"];
console.log(arr.join("="));//JS=123=false=or=today

concat

This method is used to combine two arrays or add more elements to an array then return a new array after adding.

var frontEnd = ["Javascript","React","Angular","Swift"];
var backEnd = ["Java","Ruby","Python","PHP"];
var fullStack = frontEnd.concat(backEnd);
console.log(fullStack);//['Javascript', 'React', 'Angular', 'Swift', 'Java', 'Ruby', 'Python', 'PHP']

Conclusion

In this article, we learned how to manipulate arrays in JavaScript using several methods. These methods we discussed will help you in performing simple tasks on arrays. Feel free to ask questions if you have any.

Please leave a comment or tweet me if you have any questions or recommendations! Make sure to follow me on social media!

Items.Title: 如何打击士气并摆脱最有价值的开发人员 | How to kill morale and get rid of your most valuable developers
Items.Description:
让我从几个考虑开始：本文不针对任何特定公司。 这是我以前从朋友和同事那里听到（和看到）的经历和事情的集合。

此外，该职位主要关注软件开发工作和大中型科技公司（尽管它们适用于其他公司）。 其中许多——尤其是大型企业——似乎并没有显着适应大流行和远程工作的“新常态”。

如果您想打击士气并摆脱最有价值的员工，这里有一些有用的提示。

重返办公室 (RTO) 策略不佳

在过去的两年里，大多数人一直在远程工作。 尽管如此，大多数大公司都非常活跃和活跃，其中许多都取得了创纪录的成绩！

公司已经有两年时间来准备 RTO 战略，但现在是时候回到办公室了，大多数公司似乎都没有做好规划。 在没有明确计划或对员工有任何健康问题的情况下推动日期和延误以及更多延误是不好的。 更糟糕的是，许多人甚至不提供远程选项作为一种可能性。

远程工作很有效，大多数员工都喜欢它：甚至很多曾经讨厌它的人现在也接受了它！ 远程工作为他们提供了以前无法想象的自由和灵活性。 然而，对于许多公司来说，这并不是一个选择，主要是因为经理和高管需要控制和微观管理。 公司可能有许多平庸的经理，只有少数个人贡献者。

以创纪录的成绩支付低薪

这一点甚至与大流行无关。 它是以前适用的东西，现在适用，将来也会适用。 大流行只是让它变得更加“明显”。

让我说清楚：如果你的公司有创纪录的福利，但你的基本工资低于市场中位数，那么你公司的经济可能很好，但它的灵魂已经烂透了。 你的公司价值观和原则是谎言，你的环境是有毒的，你应该受到公众的蔑视和谴责。

这将导致您最有才华的开发人员流失到其他更关心他们的员工的公司——即使他们可能并不关心那么多。 一点点就可以了。 因为这与可能是一个重要因素的薪水无关，而是关于作为员工和个人的感激之情。

减少内部晋升次数

将外部人员带入领导职位，而不考虑内部员工的人才库。 或者给员工“假促销”，让他们在其他人上岗时保持冷静。 这种行为造成了由裙带关系和办公室政治驱动的有毒工作环境。

人才、强有力的独立领导沟通、适当的认可和人员配备是推动健康工作环境的要素。 唯一可以合理地看到许多家庭成员和老朋友在一起的企业是夫妻家庭企业。 数百万美元的公司？ 红色的标志。

如果您是一名员工，并且看到一家公司的所有经理、高级领导等，都来自外部而不是内部晋升，或者有许多横向变动作为晋升出售（例如，技术管理阶梯之间的变化）。 然后，跑！ 你在该公司不会有清晰的成长路径。 当然，会有内部提拔，但很快你就会发现，大多数提拔的人并不是你期望看到的。 或者他们是典型的棕鼻子员工。 挫折迟早会接管你。

成为老大哥

大流行期间发生的一件可怕的事情是一些公司需要跟踪员工是否在远程工作（因为对于某些人来说，按时履行承诺似乎不足以证明这一点。）

许多人最终添加了（更多）软件来监视员工：他们浏览了哪些网站、他们按了哪些键、计算机空闲了多长时间……这些策略的副作用是对员工机器的性能影响。 速度较慢的计算机、持续的 VPN 问题、笔记本电脑难以运行简单的命令……即使在最先进的机器上也是如此。

我已经看到最新的 MacBook Pro 在简单的复制粘贴中苦苦挣扎，因为它必须经过无数次检测和过滤才能发生。 现在想象一下在那台计算机上运行构建或测试。 可以在几秒钟内完成的工作现在需要几分钟。 经理和主管会觉得获得了更多信息，但这会耗费公司的时间。 时间就是金钱（尤其是开发人员的时间。）

延长更换电脑所需的时间

这种说法可能听起来微不足道，但这是一个现实：开发机器很快就会变老。 并且通过对前一点采取的措施，它们变老的速度更快。 那么有什么比说现在您必须等待 5-6 年而不是等待 2-3 年更换笔记本电脑更好的公告呢？

考虑到软件开发人员目前的任期（2-4 年后换工作似乎是常态），大多数员工永远不会在您的公司看到第二台笔记本电脑。 离开的原因之一可能是机器速度非常慢。

快速的计算机会影响生产力。 高效率让员工对自己的工作和自己感觉更好。 延迟一个人完成工作的适当工具只会增加挫败感并驱使人们离开。

设定不切实际的时间表

一个项目迟到并不是什么不寻常的事情。 发生这种情况的原因有很多，其中大多数并不是可怕的迹象。 问题在于它何时成为一种习惯。

导致总是迟到的持续激进的时间表是计划不周和管理不善的标志。 它们会对员工的士气产生负面影响：它们会造成流失和倦怠，很快就会有一种消极的情绪占据员工的心。

开发人员可能会忍受一次，也许两次。 在那之后，它们将开始像苍蝇一样下落。 当公司需要做的只是培训或更换团队中的一个人时，公司将失去整个团队（以及与他们相关的公司和产品知识）。

结论

其中许多观点适用于技术以外的公司以及软件开发人员以外的职位，但感觉它们在 IT 行业中变得越来越普遍。

不幸的是，对于员工（对于公司来说也是幸运的），其中许多要点只有在您进入公司后才能看到。 候选人可以在面试期间提出一些可以对他们有所了解的问题，但是很容易回避这些问题（红旗）或者只是在这些问题上撒谎（同样，直到内部才能确定，但​​一次更大的旗帜学到了。）

如果您是员工，请注意此类行为。 如果您是一家公司……请，请，请：不惜一切代价避免它们。



原文:

Let me start with a couple of considerations: this article doesn't aim at any particular company. It is a collection of previous experiences and things I've heard (and seen) from friends and coworkers.

Also, the post focuses mainly on software development jobs and mid-to-large-sized tech companies (although they apply to others). Many of which –especially the big ones– don't seem to have adapted significantly to the "new normal" of the pandemic and remote working.

Here are some helpful tips if you want to kill morale and get rid of your most valuable employees.

Have a poor Return To Office (RTO) strategy

The COVID-19 pandemic has hit the world hard. Most people have been working remotely for the past two years. Still, most big companies are very much alive and kicking, and many of them with record results!

Companies have had two years to prepare an RTO strategy, but now that the time is coming to go back to the office, most seem to have done poorly planning it. Pushing through dates and delays and more delays without a clear plan or any health concerns for the employees is not good. And what's worse, many don't even offer a remote option as a possibility.

Remote work works, and most employees love it: even many people who used to hate it have embraced it now! Remote work gives them freedom and flexibility that they couldn't dream of before. And yet, it's not an option for many companies, mainly due to managers' and executives' need for control and micromanagement. The company may have many mediocre managers and just a few individual contributors.

Pay low salaries with record results

This point doesn't even have to do with the pandemic; it is something that applied before, applies now, and will apply in the future too. The pandemic just made it more "obvious."

Let me say it clearly: if your company has record-breaking benefits, but your base pay is below the market median, your company's economy may be excellent, but its soul is rotten to the core. Your company values and principles are a lie, your environment is toxic, and you deserve nothing but public scorn and reprimands.

These will result in losing your most talented developers to other companies that will care more for their people –even when they may not care that much more. Just a little bit will do. Because it's not about the pay, which may be a significant factor, it's about feeling appreciated as an employee and a person.

Reduce the number of internal promotions

Bring people from the outside for leadership positions without considering the talented pool of employees inside. Or give "fake promotions" to employees to keep them calm while other people go up. This behavior creates a toxic work environment driven by nepotism and office politics. 

Talent, strong independent leadership communications, and appropriate recognition and staffing are, among other things, what should drive a healthy work environment. The only business where it is reasonable to see many family members and long-time friends together is a mom-and-pop family business. A multimillion-dollar company? Red flag.

If you are an employee and see a company where all the managers, senior leaders, etc., come from the outside instead of internal promotion or have many lateral moves sold as promotions (e.g., change between technical-management ladder). Then, run! You will not have a clear growth path in that company. Of course, there will be internal promotions, but soon you will notice that most promoted people are not who you expected to see going up. Or they are the stereotypical brown-nosed employees. Frustration will take over you sooner than later.

Become the Big Brother

A terrible thing that happened over the pandemic was the need of some companies to track that employees were working remotely (because it seems like delivering their commitments on time was not enough proof of that for some people.)

Many ended up adding (more) software to spy on the employees: what sites they browse, what keys they press, how much time the computer is idle… A side effect of these policies is the performance impact on the employees' machines. Slower computers, continuous VPN issues, laptops struggling to run simple commands… even on state-of-the-art machines.

I've seen the latest MacBook Pro struggling with a simple copy-paste because it had to go through numerous detections and filters before it happened. Now imagine running builds or tests on that computer. Work that could be done in seconds now takes minutes. Managers and executives will feel empowered with more information, but it costs the company time. And time is money (especially developers' time.)

Extend the time needed to replace computers

This claim may sound petty, but it is a reality: development machines get old fast. And with the measures taken on the previous point, they get old even faster. So what better announcement than saying that instead of waiting for 2–3 years to change laptop, now you'll have to wait 5–6 years?

Considering the current tenure among software developers (changing jobs seems the norm after 2–4 years), most employees will never see a second laptop at your company. And one of the reasons for leaving may be having a painfully slow machine.

A fast computer impacts productivity. And being productive makes employees feel better about their work and themselves. Delaying the proper tools for a person to do their job will only add frustration and drive people away.

Set unrealistic timelines

Being late for a project is not something out of the ordinary. It could happen for many reasons, and most of them are not a sign of something terrible. The problem is when it becomes a habit.

Continuous aggressive timelines that lead to always being late are a sign of poor planning and bad management. And they will impact negatively on the employees' morale: they create churn and burnout, and soon a negative feeling will possess the employees.

Developers may put up with it once, maybe twice. After that, they will start dropping like flies. The company will lose whole teams (and the corporate and product knowledge associated with them) when all it needs to do is to train or replace a single person in the group.

Conclusion

Many of these points apply to companies outside of tech and to positions other than software developers, but it feels like they are becoming more pervasive in the IT industry.

Unfortunately for employees (and luckily for companies), many of these points are only visible after you are inside the company. Candidates can ask questions during the interview that will shed some light on them, but it would be easy to dodge the questions (red flag) or just flat lie about them (again, not possible to determine until inside, but an even bigger flag once learned.)

If you are an employee, be on the lookout for this type of behavior. If you are a company… please, please, please: avoid them at all costs.

和两个数字应用程序


本周我们创建了一个 Sum Two Numbers 应用程序。 用户在表格中输入两个数字，求和的结果显示在反应中。

创建 SumTwoNum 组件； 我们必须 ：

  • 创建两个状态来保存每个数字的值。

  • 创建一个计算和更新总和的计算函数。
  • 为每个数字创建两个输入并处理事件。
  • 代码
    import React , { useState } from ' react ' ; const SumTwoNum = () => { const [ number1 , setNumber1 ] = useState (); const [ number2 , setNumber2 ] = useState (); const [ summation , setSummation ] = useState ( 0 ); function calculate () { setSummation ( number1 + number2 ); } return ( < div > < h2 > Sum Two Numbers </ h2 > < input placeholder = "First Number" type = "number" value = { number1 } onChange = { ( ev ) => setNumber1 ( + ev . target . value ) } /> < input placeholder = "Second Number" type = "number" value = { number2 } onChange = { ( ev ) => setNumber2 ( + ev . target . value ) } /> < br /> < button onClick = { calculate } > Sum Two Numbers </ button > < p > Summation : { summation || " 0 " } </ p > </ div > ); } export default SumTwoNum ;

原文:

Mohamed Khaled Yousef

Posted on May 31

Hello developer!, glad to see you again.

This is a react-curve, open source project where I can share and start creating small UI components in the way I understood the concepts to build large scale projects .

Sum Two Numbers App


This week we created a Sum Two Numbers app. The user enter two numbers in a form and result of the summation is shown in react.

To create a SumTwoNum component; We have to :

  • Create two states that hold the value of each number.
  • Create a state that holds the summation value with an initial value equal to zero.
  • Create a calculate function that calculates and updates the summation.
  • Create two inputs and handle events for each number.
  • onClick submit button, calculate method is fired and do summation.

Code 

import React, {useState} from 'react'; const SumTwoNum = () => { const [number1, setNumber1] = useState(); const [number2, setNumber2] = useState(); const [summation, setSummation] = useState(0); function calculate() { setSummation(number1 + number2); } return ( <div> <h2>Sum Two Numbers</h2> <input placeholder="First Number" type="number" value={number1} onChange={(ev) => setNumber1(+ev.target.value)} /> <input placeholder="Second Number" type="number" value={number2} onChange={(ev) => setNumber2(+ev.target.value)} /> <br /> <button onClick={calculate}>Sum Two Numbers</button> <p>Summation : {summation || "0"}</p> </div> );
} export default SumTwoNum;

Conclusion

Thank you for reading and any contribution is more than welcome in the threads below!

Items.Title: 编码和多动症 - 多动症大脑 | Coding and ADHD - ADHD Brains
Items.Description:
对我来说，我的注意力缺陷/多动障碍 (ADHD) 和我编写代码的能力是不可逆转的联系在一起的。 多年来我一直怀疑自己患有多动症，但直到 2021 年 9 月才通过正式的诊断程序。我去接受测试的主要原因之一是当我对编码问题感兴趣时，我可能会失去 6 多个小时，而且经常会错过会议和用餐。

我有多动症混合型，这意味着我有多动和冲动症状以及注意力不集中的症状。 除其他外，我还在努力：

  • 坐立不安/不安
  • 不耐烦/沮丧
  • 超焦点
  • 反刍
  • 时间失明
  • 持续关注
  • 工作记忆力差
  • 拖延
  • 缺乏对细节的关注

多动症大脑

很多人说得好，但我觉得我太关注了。 我希望能够关注一件事。 仅仅因为我或其他人认为它应该很重要，所以我的大脑专注于某件事会很棒。 事实上，我坐着不动，没有任何有趣的事情发生，这对我来说是非常痛苦的。 而不是“注意力缺陷”，它应该被称为“注意力调节”或“执行功能”/多动障碍。

编码可以给多动症大脑提供他们渴望的那种刺激。 每个大脑都有奖励途径来加强学习——如果你按下一个按钮并立即得到你真正喜欢的东西，你的大脑会想再次按下按钮。 多动症大脑以不同的方式处理按钮。 如果他们不知道按下按钮会给他们真正喜欢的奖励，他们就会挣扎。 他们很难找到奖励足够好的按钮，让他们想再次按下它。 一旦他们找到他们喜欢的按钮，他们通常会按太多次。 目前的理论是，我们的学习途径更快地消耗多巴胺，我们的大脑不会像其他大脑那样将葡萄糖转化为多巴胺和血清素。 换句话说，我们一直在寻找能给我们足够好的奖励的东西，而一旦找到它们，我们就会不理会这些东西。

对我们来说，这些奖励的部分吸引力在于新颖性。 患有多动症的人在对某事感兴趣时也不会停下来质疑它。 所以他们经常可以在没有意识到他们应该很努力的情况下取得伟大的成就，但通常也有很多爱好和项目，这些爱好和项目是开始然后放弃的。

幸运的是，编码不仅是一项需要不断学习新事物的创造性工作，而且一旦解决了一个问题，总会有一个全新的尝试。

结论

就个人而言，我永远不会将 ADHD 描述为一种超能力。 是的，这就是为什么我可以在一夜之间学习我感兴趣的编码概念的原因。 人们似乎很欣赏我的大脑想要不断培养兴趣并直接潜入项目深处的方式。 这也是为什么我不能在不哭的情况下尝试算法编码挑战，经常尝试在没有测试的情况下一次更改太多东西，并且已经在这个博客上写了 40 分钟而不是吃午饭。

至此，本系列的其余部分将讨论应对我在使用 ADHD 编码时遇到的四个主要挑战的策略：

  • 无法启动
  • 无法继续
  • 停不下来
  • 不记得


原文:

To me, my Attention-Deficit / Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) and my ability to write code are irrevocably linked. I had suspected I had ADHD for years, but only went through the formal diagnosis process in September 2021. One of the primary reasons I went to get tested was when I was interested in a coding problem, I could lose 6+ hours, often missing meetings and meals.

I have ADHD combined type, which means I have hyperactive and impulsive symptoms as well as inattentive symptoms. Among other things, I struggle with:

  • fidgeting/restlessness
  • impatience/frustration
  • hyperfocus
  • rumination
  • time blindness
  • Rejection Sensitive Dysphoria
  • sustaining attention
  • poor working memory
  • procrastination
  • lack of attention to detail

ADHD Brains

Many people have said it better, but I feel like I have too much attention. I would love to be able to pay attention to one thing. It would be great for my brain to focus on something just because I or other people think it should be important. In fact, it is physically painful for me to sit still with nothing interesting happening. Rather than "Attention-Deficit," it should be called something like "Attention-Regulation" or "Executive Function" / Hyperactivity Disorder.

Coding can give ADHD brains exactly the kind of stimulation they crave. Every brain has reward pathways to reinforce learning - if you hit a button and immediately get a thing you really like, your brain will want to hit the button again. If the payoff is random, like fixing a bug and trying a bunch of different things before you find the solution, then the reward seems even better to brains.

ADHD brains approach the buttons differently. They struggle with pressing the button if they don't know it'll give them a reward they really like. They have a hard time finding buttons with rewards good enough to make them want to press it again. Once they find a button they like, they often press it way too many times. The current theory is that our learning pathways use up dopamine faster and our brains don't convert glucose to dopamine and serotonin as well as other brains. In other words, we're constantly searching for things that will give us good enough rewards and bad at leaving those things alone once we find them.

For us, part of the appeal of those rewards is novelty. People with ADHD also don't stop to question it when they're interested in something. So they can often achieve great things without realizing they were supposed to be hard, but also usually have many hobbies and projects that were started and then abandoned.

Luckily for us, not only is coding a creative endeavor that involves constantly learning new things, but also once one problem is solved, there's always a brand new one to try.

Conclusion

Personally, I would never describe ADHD as a super power. Yes, it's why I could learn coding concepts I'm interested in seemingly overnight. People seem to admire the way my brain wants to be constantly picking up hobbies and diving straight into the deep end of projects. It's also why I cannot attempt an algorithm coding challenge without crying, often try to change too many things at once without testing, and have been typing this blog for 40 minutes instead of eating lunch.

To that point, the rest of this series will be about strategies for dealing with the four main challenges I experience while coding with ADHD:

  • Can't Start
  • Can't Keep Going
  • Can't Stop
  • Can't Remember

Items.Title: 数据库迁移 (6.2) | Database Migrations (6.2)
Items.Description:
最近发现了如何迁移/更改应用程序数据库。

让我们看一下我们新的迁移文件的内容：

 class CreateArticles < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0] def change create_table :articles do |t| t.string :title t.text :body t.timestamps end end end

对create_table的调用指定应该如何构造articles表。 默认情况下， create_table方法会添加一个id列作为自增主键。 所以表中的第一条记录的id为 1，下一条记录的id为 2，依此类推。

在create_table块内，定义了两列： title和body 。 这些是由生成器添加的，因为我们将它们包含在我们的生成命令中（ bin/rails generate model Article title:string body:text ）。

该块的最后一行是对t.timestamps的调用。 此方法定义了两个名为created_at和updated_at的附加列。 正如我们将看到的，Rails 将为我们管理这些，在我们创建或更新模型对象时设置值。



原文:

Recently found out how to migrate/alter applications database.

Let's take a look at the contents of our new migration file:

class CreateArticles < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0]
  def change
    create_table :articles do |t|
      t.string :title
      t.text :body

      t.timestamps
    end
  end
end

The call to create_table specifies how the articles table should be constructed. By default, the create_table method adds an id column as an auto-incrementing primary key. So the first record in the table will have an id of 1, the next record will have an id of 2, and so on.

Inside the block for create_table, two columns are defined: title and body. These were added by the generator because we included them in our generate command (bin/rails generate model Article title:string body:text).

On the last line of the block is a call to t.timestamps. This method defines two additional columns named created_at and updated_at. As we will see, Rails will manage these for us, setting the values when we create or update a model object.
